text: **Anna:** No, that's a great {idea|考え/アイデア}.
**Anna:** Rick, I think we should use this as the {opening|開始/最初} shot.
**Penelope:** Anna, did Miss Weaver give us {instructions|指示/説明書} for covering the car show?
**Anna:** She did.
**Anna:** She said that we need to show lots of {cars|自動車}.
**Anna:** She said we
**Anna:** need to {interview|インタビューする} people and she also said to have fun those were her exact words yeah she said have
**Anna:** Have fun Anna!
**Anna:** And she is the {boss|上司/リーダー}.
**Kurt:** Okay, Rick, are you are you ready?
**Penelope:** Okay.
**Anna:** Hello, I'm Anna Mateo.
**Professor Bot:** Welcome to the Washington Car Show.
**Anna:** People all over town are saying that if you like cars, this is the place to be.
**Anna:** So let's
**Anna:** See what people are talking about.
**Anna:** How was that?
**Penelope:** Okay?
**Anna:** Is that good?
**Anna:** We often need to tell others what someone else said.
**Anna:** There are two ways to do this.
**Anna:** One is to use the person's exact words and use {quotation|引用} marks.
**Anna:** We call this {direct|直接の} speech.
**Penelope:** For example, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver had said, you need to show lots of cars.
**Anna:** The other way is to talk about what someone said.
**Anna:** We call this {reported|報告された} speed
**Anna:** Anna tells Penelope, She said that we need to show lots of cars.
**Anna:** In this sentence, she is the {subject|主語}.
**Anna:** Said is the {reporting|報告する} verb.
**Anna:** That is the {conjunction|接続詞|/kənˈdʒʌŋkʃən/|And is a conjunction.}
**Anna:** And we need to show lots of {cars|車|/kɑːrz/|I have three cars.} is the reporting clause.
**Anna:** Keep {watching|見守る/観察する|/ˈwɑːtʃɪŋ/|Please keep watching the movie.} and listen for places where Anna talks about what someone else said.
**Anna:** This car show has many {styles|スタイル/様式|/staɪlz/|Fashion styles change quickly.} of {vehicles|乗り物/車両|/ˈviːɪkəlz/|Vehicles are important for transportation.}

**Anna:** There are {utility|実用的な|/juːˈtɪləti/|Utility vehicles are very practical.} vehicles, {classic|古典的な/伝統的な|/ˈklæsɪk/|Classic cars are beautiful.} cars, trucks, {sports|スポーツの|/spɔːrts/|Sports cars are fast.} cars, and everyday vehicles.
**Penelope:** Okay, I think we got good interviews.
**Anna:** Me too.
**Anna:** People all had very {different|異なる/違う|/ˈdɪfərənt/|We have different opinions.} opinions, but they all said they loved cars.
**Penelope:** Anna, look!
**Penelope:** A robot!
**Anna:** We have to use the {robot|ロボット|/ˈroʊbɑːt/|The robot can help us.} in the show.
**Robot:** What is your first name?
**Penelope:** Anna!
**Robot:** You know, what an effort.
**Anna:** What an effort to {robot|ロボット|/ˈroʊbɑːt/|The robot danced.} dance.
**Anna:** How about a round of {applause|拍手|/əˈplɔːz/|Let's give applause for the winner.} for Anna?
**Anna:** I've never seen anyone twice so long.
**Anna:** Penelope, did you hear that?
**Anna:** Hank the robot said that he has never seen anyone dance like me.
**Penelope:** Uh I think a lot of people here are saying that, Anna.
**Anna:** That was fun.
**Anna:** You know what someone told me?
**Anna:** You can {test drive|試乗する|/test draɪv/|We can test drive the new car.} a Jaguar at this car show.
**Anna:** Now that sounds fun.
**Anna:** Let's go.
**Penelope:** Okay.
**Anna:** This {course|コース/経路|/kɔːrs/|The race course is long.} is smooth.
**Anna:** So you can drive
**Anna:** Faster.
**Anna:** He said that I can't drive, unfortunately.
**Anna:** {Awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしく面白い|/ˈɔːsəm/|That was an awesome party!}
**Anna:** Reverse {camera|カメラ|/ˈkæmərə/|My camera is new.} there.
**Anna:** Nice {big|大きい/大規模な|/bɪɡ/|This is a big house.}
**Anna:** Gosh, {reverse|逆の/戻す|/rɪˈvɜːrs/|Please reverse the car.}
**Anna:** That was {great|素晴らしい/素晴らしく良い|/ɡreɪt/|We had a great time.}
**Penelope:** Okay.
**Anna:** This is {awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしく面白い|/ˈɔːsəm/|That was an awesome concert!}
**Kurt:** Ready to go over the {roller coaster|ジェットコースター|/ˈroʊlər ˈkoʊstər/|Let's ride the roller coaster.}?
**Kurt:** I'm ready.
**Kurt:** Alright, here we go.
**Anna:** That's {awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしく面白い|/ˈɔːsəm/|What an awesome experience!}
**Anna:** And then here we go down the {finish line|ゴールライン|/ˈfɪnɪʃ laɪn/|He crossed the finish line first.}
**Anna:** {Jaguar|ジャガー（車種）|/ˈdʒæɡwər/|Jaguar is a luxury car brand.} takes the {checkered|チェック柄の|/ˈtʃekərd/|A checkered flag signals the end.} flat.
**Anna:** That was that was {awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしく面白い|/ˈɔːsəm/|This is an awesome adventure!}
**Anna:** I just want to go one more time.
**Anna:** I promise that's it.
**Anna:** Just one more time.
**Anna:** This {course|コース/経路|/kɔːrs/|We ran the entire course.} is {bumpy|でこぼこの|/ˈbʌmpi/|The road was very bumpy.} and {uneven|不均一な|/ʌnˈiːvən/|The ground was uneven.}
**Anna:** I was told that I could drive this course, so I am
**Anna:** Okay, Rick, are you {rolling|回転している/撮影している|/ˈroʊlɪŋ/|The camera is rolling.}?
**Anna:** Awesome.
**Anna:** Okay, this course is {uneven|不均一な|/ʌnˈiːvən/|The surface was uneven.}, but I think the ride will be smooth.
**Anna:** This {hill|丘/坂|/hɪl/|We climbed the steep hill.} is really {steep|急な|/stiːp/|The road was very steep.}
**Anna:** Oh my gosh.
**Anna:** I can't tell you how much fun this is.
**Anna:** Penelope, that was so much fun.
**Anna:** I think I need to go again.
**Penelope:** Uh Anna?
**Penelope:** I think we have enough test drive video.
**Penelope:** Ms.
**Anna:** {Weaver|人名（姓）|/ˈwiːvər/|Weaver is my friend.} said to get a lot.
**Anna:** So I think I should {go again|再び行く|/ɡoʊ əˈɡeɪn/|I will go again tomorrow.}
**Anna:** Rick, I'm gonna go again.
**Anna:** Till next time.
**Penelope:** But I want to get {lunch|昼食|/lʌntʃ/|Let's have lunch together.}
**Anna:** Penelope said she wants to go to {lunch|昼食|/lʌntʃ/|We often have lunch at noon.}, but I don't think that's going to happen soon.
